name: Vape / E-Cigarette
description: Vaping devices, e-cigarettes, electronic hookahs, and similar battery-powered devices. These devices are subject to strict rules due to their lithium batteries and flammable e-liquid.
tsaGuideline: E-cigarettes and vaping devices are allowed in carry-on baggage and on your person but NOT in checked baggage. This is due to the risk of the battery activating and causing a fire in the cargo hold. E-liquids in carry-on must follow the 3-1-1 liquids rule (containers up to 3.4 oz / 100 ml in a quart-sized bag).
iataGuideline: Electronic cigarettes must be in the cabin baggage or on the person. They must not be used or charged on board. E-liquid containers must follow liquids regulations.
capacityLimit: E-liquid: 100 ml per container in carry-on (3-1-1 rule)
restrictions: MUST be in carry-on or on your person — strictly prohibited in checked baggage
Cannot be used on board any aircraft
Cannot be charged on board the aircraft
E-liquid in carry-on must follow 3-1-1 liquids rule (max 100 ml containers)
Battery must be protected against accidental activation
Some countries have bans on vaping products entirely (e.g., Thailand, Singapore)

airlineRules.notes: Carry-on only. No use or charging on board.
Carry-on or on person only. Prohibited in checked baggage.
Carry-on only. No smoking or vaping on board.
Permitted in carry-on. Cannot be used on board.
Carry-on only. Note: Singapore bans sale and use of e-cigarettes — do not bring them into Singapore.

faqs.question: Can I pack a vape in my checked luggage?
faqs.answer: No. Vaping devices and e-cigarettes are strictly prohibited in checked baggage on all airlines and by TSA regulations. They must be in your carry-on bag or on your person. Violating this rule can result in fines.

faqs.question: Can I bring e-liquid on a plane?
faqs.answer: Yes, e-liquid (vape juice) in your carry-on must follow the TSA 3-1-1 rule: containers must be 3.4 oz (100 ml) or less and must fit in one quart-sized clear plastic bag. You can put larger quantities in checked luggage.

faqs.question: Can I vape on a plane?
faqs.answer: No. Vaping and using e-cigarettes is prohibited on all commercial flights worldwide. Attempting to vape on board is treated the same as smoking and can result in fines and being banned by the airline.

faqs.question: Can I bring my vape to countries where vaping is illegal?
faqs.answer: No. Countries like Thailand, Singapore, India, Brazil, and several others have banned vaping products entirely. Bringing a vape into these countries could result in confiscation, fines, or arrest.
